meta.notes: Phase 1 draft questionnaire answers derived from provided project documents. Line numbers for PDFs refer to extracted text lines. | Phase 2: incorporated confirmations; remaining unknowns are non-blocking.

sources.source_id: Yana Gut project - MultimodalText Deep Research A Judge-Ready Diet-Microbiome-Metabolites-Cognition Simulator App.md

answers.value: A judge-ready Gut–Brain Simulator web app for the project “Modeling Diet-Driven Microbiome and Neurotransmitter Pathways Influencing Cognitive Performance”. Users adjust diet inputs and see simulated microbiome shifts, metabolite proxies, and cognitive performance signals, plus real pilot (n=66) association charts and 1-page export reports.

evidence.snippet: Modeling Diet-Driven Microbiome and Neurotransmitter Pathways
This approach allows for simulation of how changes in diet propagate through biological systems  and influence cognitive outcomes.

- **Pilot Results (n=66)**: upload/select the real CSV → auto-generate charts and a clean summary table.   - **Simulator**: nutrient inputs (manual or FoodData Central lookup) → predicted microbiome s
Your “Export 1-page report (PDF/HTML)” should include: - Inputs (diet features),

answers.value: Enable Yana to demonstrate a transparent, reproducible diet→microbiome→metabolites→cognition simulation with clear anti-leakage guardrails and an exportable 1-page judge report.

answers.value: Communicating the mechanistic pathway and scientific rigor is hard with static slides. A live simulator + real pilot association charts reduce judge skepticism and make the project easier to understand quickly.

value: No medical diagnosis or treatment recommendations.
No collection of new human subject data (no surveys, no stool samples).
No live training of large multi-omics ML models inside the app.

value: Must remain consistent with a computational project using public de-identified datasets and a separate de-identified teen pilot benchmark dataset (association charts only; not used to fit or tune simulator parameters).
Must be ready for live STEM Showcase judging and avoid language implying causality/proof.
Must keep data de-identified.

value: Flow 1 (Judge walkthrough): open app → Pilot Results (n=66) → Methods & Rigor → run a Simulator scenario → Export 1-page report.
Flow 2 (Data check): upload/select pilot CSV → verify summary stats → view correlation plots → export pilot summary.
Flow 3 (Scenario exploration): adjust diet inputs → compare two scenarios → inspect microbiome/metabolite proxy differences → export comparison.
